codeReview.summary: Clean removal of SVG icon rasterization and icon-specific code across 3 files (154 lines deleted, 18 added). Compositor now has zero Content:: references, zero SVG/icon references, and is a thin event loop (459 lines: config → font setup → render loop). Protocol IconConfig struct and MSG_ICON_CONFIG/MSG_IMG_ICON_CONFIG constants removed. Init no longer loads doc-icon.svg or img-icon.svg from 9p, and the font_buf tuple was correctly simplified from 9 to 5 elements. All verification greps return zero matches. Worker followed the procedure correctly: baseline tests first, implementation, full test suite (1,764 pass), QEMU visual verification (screenshot confirmed working display).

issues.description: Extra blank line left at compositor main.rs line 173-174 where icon message consumption code was removed. Two consecutive blank lines between the image config block and the CpuBackend construction section. Cosmetic only.

Extra blank line left at init main.rs line 567-568 where icon message sending code was removed. Two consecutive blank lines between the image config send and the cross-process channels section. Cosmetic only.

Stale SVG/icon references remain in other files outside this feature's scope: (1) build.rs:120 comment says 'SVG rasterization' in render library description — should be updated since SVG was removed from render in a prior feature. (2) drawing/palette.rs:45-46 defines CHROME_ICON constant with comment 'SVG icon tint' — this constant is now unused (zero references). (3) test/tests/drawing.rs:4862 has comment '(SVG tests moved to test/tests/svg.rs)'. These are pre-existing or cross-feature cleanup items, not regressions from this feature.

sharedStateObservations.observation: AGENTS.md references 'compositor, SVG' in the Work Procedure (system/services/compositor/ description says 'renderer, damage tracking, compositing, SVG'). After this feature, the compositor has zero SVG knowledge. The skill's file listing for compositor should drop the SVG reference.

AGENTS.md says 'Atomic removal: PathCmd/PathCmdKind from scene, Path rendering + svg.rs from render, and SVG from compositor MUST be removed in one atomic commit.' This constraint was satisfied across the milestone's feature sequence (render-new-content-types + compositor-svg-removal). The constraint itself is now historical — it could be removed or marked as completed to avoid confusing future workers.

The os-worker skill file (SKILL.md) lists 'system/services/compositor/ — renderer, damage tracking, compositing, SVG' and 'SVG → system/test/tests/svg.rs' as test locations. After this mission, SVG is fully removed from compositor and render. These references should be updated to reflect the new architecture.

No .factory/library/ directory exists for this project. The worker's transcript shows it read .factory/library but found nothing. There's no architecture knowledge base file documenting the rendering pipeline's current state post-cleanup. A library entry describing the new content-agnostic compositor architecture (geometric content types: FillRect, Glyphs, Image; compositor as pure pixel pump) would help future workers.

summary: PASS. Clean, focused removal of all SVG icon rasterization and icon-specific code from compositor (main.rs), init service, and protocol library. 154 lines deleted across 3 files. The compositor is now a content-agnostic pixel pump at 459 lines with zero Content:: references and zero SVG/icon knowledge. All 1,764 tests pass, QEMU visual verification confirmed working display pipeline. Three non-blocking cosmetic issues: two extra blank lines left by code deletion and stale SVG references in files outside this feature's scope. Worker followed the os-worker skill procedure correctly and produced thorough verification evidence.
